title: rubus pallidus
intro: Rubus pallidus, also known as the pale bramble or pale raspberry, is a species of thorny shrub in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to southern Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Rubus pallidus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying the plant material to create a powder that can be used in capsules or as a topical ointment.
